name: PINNACLE WEST CAPITAL CORP
business: Pinnacle West is an investor-owned electric utility holding company based in Phoenix, Arizona with consolidated assets of approximately $30 billion. We derive essentially all of our revenues and earnings from our principal subsidiary, APS. APS is Arizona's largest and longest-serving electric company and generates safe, affordable electricity in 11 of Arizona's 15 counties.

exposures.sentence: Our reportable business segment is our regulated electricity segment, which consists of traditional regulated retail and wholesale electricity businesses (primarily electric service to Native Load customers) and related activities, and includes electricity generation, transmission, and distribution.
Palo Verde is a 3-unit nuclear power plant located approximately 50 miles west of Phoenix, Arizona.
APS has a diverse portfolio of existing and planned resources, including biomass, biogas, coal, energy storage, geothermal, natural gas, solar, and wind.

APS is utilizing grid-scale energy storage projects to meet customer reliability requirements, increase renewable utilization, and further our understanding of how storage works with other advanced technologies and the grid.
APS has six natural gas power plants located throughout Arizona.
APS owns 63% of Four Corners Units 4 and 5.
To prioritize reliability and meet substantial growth in residential and commercial energy needs, APS has developed a future-focused, strategic transmission plan (the Ten-Year Transmission Plan).

As large load customers, such as data centers and large manufacturers, have continued to grow as a proportion of our business, we have updated our procedures with respect to estimates of unbilled revenues for our customer classes.

The capital expenditures table presented in the Liquidity and Capital Resources section of Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations in Part II, Item 7 of this report includes new APS transmission projects, along with other transmission costs for upgrades and replacements, including those for data center and semi-conductor manufacturing development.
We are subject to risk related to cybersecurity, IT systems, and unauthorized access to our systems that could adversely affect our business and financial condition.
Wildfire safety remains a critical focus for APS and other utilities.

To address the rapid advancement of AI technology risks and opportunities, APS has developed an AI strategy to responsibly utilize AI to advance our business strategy, enhance customer and employee experiences, and optimize operational reliability.

APS continues to pursue and implement advanced grid technologies, including transmission and distribution system technologies and digital meters enabling two-way communications between the utility and its customers.

catalysts.sentence: The hearing for this rate case is currently scheduled to begin in May 2026.
The new pipeline is expected to be operational by late 2029 and will be owned and operated by a third-party.

catalysts.event: Palo Verde Unit 2 remaining lease expiration
catalysts.sentence: The remaining lease for approximately 5.2% of Unit 2 expires in 2033.
APS's next IRP is in process and due to be filed with the ACC in August 2026.
the addition of eight combustion turbines (approximately 397 MW total) at Redhawk, which are expected to be in service in 2028.

catalysts.event: Cross-Tie Project Record of Decision non-appealable
catalysts.sentence: On December 18, 2025, the Department of Interior Bureau of Land Management issued a Record of Decision permitting the development of Cross-Tie Project, which became non-appealable in late January 2026.
APS is a funding party to the implementation phase of Markets+ and expects to go live in the market in October 2027.
